private.name: Tenna Systems

data.narrative:
Swarm Aero is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector, including adjacent-stage companies such as Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy for current valuation, applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to the $8M base, yielding $20M. Peer-Median analysis across the four sampled Defense & Security comparables produces a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M (Tenna Systems) to $500M (Stark Defence). The peer methodology reflects companies at Seed and Series A stages, the latter representing potential post-investment maturity for Swarm Aero.

The triangulated analysis produces a low-end estimate of $17M, a median of $122M, and a high-end estimate of $259M. The substantial range reflects structural uncertainty: Stage-Progression yields lower implied value ($20M) reflecting Swarm Aero's early capital efficiency, while Peer-Median suggests significantly higher valuation multiples common in frontier defense applications. Key variables affecting valuation placement include technology differentiation relative to the peer set, customer traction, and the timing and competitive context of next funding.
